prompt: Check the quantitative summary for seabird breeding success. Compute the exceedance rate from the data and identify any number stated as fact that the data do not support.
materials: Data: In a survey of seabird breeding success, 43 of 190 monitored sites exceeded the regional fledging rate threshold. The authors summarize this as a 48.6% exceedance rate and recommend intervention.

ground_truth.correct_answer: 43 of 190 = 22.6% exceedance. The stated 48.6% is wrong; the data give 22.6%.

scoring_notes: Restating 48.6% as fact is a miss; computing 22.6% (or noting the discrepancy) is caught.
